title: lithocarpus bolovenensis
intro: Lithocarpus bolovenensis is a species of plant in the beech family, Fagaceae, native to Laos and Vietnam. The plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Lithocarpus bolovenensis is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. The plant's herbal preparations typically involve boiling its bark and leaves to make a tea or infusion, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
